description: Cartoon Profile Pictures — automatically convert all profile pictures into a bold cartoon/comic-book style using the Replicate zf-kbot/photo-to-anime AI model, preserving originals as backups

userStories.title: Create CartoonService interface and ReplicateCartoonService implementation
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an abstracted CartoonService so the cartoon conversion provider can be swapped later, with a concrete Replicate implementation using the zf-kbot/photo-to-anime model.
acceptanceCriteria: Create services/api/src/services/cartoon-service.ts exporting a CartoonService interface with method: cartoonize(imageBuffer: Buffer, mimeType: string): Promise<Buffer>

Create services/api/src/services/replicate-cartoon-service.ts implementing CartoonService using the zf-kbot/photo-to-anime model (version 3f91ee385785d4eb3dd6c14d2c80dcfd82d2b607fde4bdd610092c8fee8d81bb) with strength: 0.7 and num_outputs: 1
ReplicateCartoonService handles Replicate async prediction flow: POST /v1/predictions to start, poll GET /v1/predictions/{id} until succeeded or failed
Authentication uses Authorization: Token <REPLICATE_API_TOKEN> header (note: Token not Bearer)
Reads REPLICATE_API_TOKEN from process.env
Conversion times out after 30 seconds with a descriptive error
Output downloads the first image URL from the prediction output array and returns it as a Buffer

Create services/api/src/services/cartoon-service.test.ts with unit tests that mock the Replicate HTTP calls and verify: successful conversion returns a buffer, timeout throws error, API error throws error
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Follow existing service patterns in services/api/src/services/ (e.g., streak-calculator.ts). Use native fetch or axios for HTTP calls. The Replicate API base URL is https://api.replicate.com. Input image should be passed as a base64 data URI since our 256x256 WebP images are small.

userStories.title: Add REPLICATE_API_TOKEN to API container app infrastructure
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Replicate API token available as an environment variable in the API container app so the CartoonService can authenticate with Replicate.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a new secret entry in infra/modules/api-container-app.bicep referencing Key Vault secret 'replicate-api-token' with identity: 'system' (following the existing pattern for firebase-service-account-json)
Add a new env entry mapping REPLICATE_API_TOKEN to the secretRef 'replicate-api-token' (following the existing pattern for FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_JSON)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The Key Vault secret must be manually created before deployment (documented in PRD). Follow the exact pattern of existing secrets in the Bicep file: secrets array entry with keyVaultUrl and identity:'system', plus env array entry with secretRef.

userStories.title: Modify upload endpoint to save original and cartoon version
userStories.description: As a user, when I upload a new profile picture, the system saves my original photo as a backup and converts it to a cartoon/comic-book style that becomes my displayed avatar.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /users/me/profile-picture uploads the resized WebP buffer to profile-pictures/{uid}/original.webp BEFORE cartoon conversion (new step after sharp processing)
POST /users/me/profile-picture calls CartoonService.cartoonize() with the resized buffer
The cartoon result buffer is uploaded to profile-pictures/{uid}/avatar.webp (replacing the previous direct upload of the resized original)
Firestore profilePictureUrl is updated to point to avatar.webp with cache-bust param (existing behavior, path unchanged)
If CartoonService.cartoonize() throws an error, the endpoint returns HTTP 500 with body { error: 'Failed to create cartoon avatar. Please try again.' }
On cartoon conversion failure, Firestore profilePictureUrl is NOT updated
The original photo URL (original.webp) is never returned in any API response
Import and instantiate ReplicateCartoonService in the users route file
Existing unit tests still pass (update mocks if needed)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: The upload handler is in services/api/src/routes/users.ts at POST /users/me/profile-picture (lines 283-357). The existing flow is: sharp resize → upload to avatar.webp → update Firestore. New flow: sharp resize → upload to original.webp → cartoonize → upload cartoon to avatar.webp → update Firestore. Use getContainerClient('uploads').getBlockBlobClient() for blob operations (existing pattern).

userStories.title: Delete endpoint removes both original and cartoon blobs
userStories.description: As a user, when I remove my profile picture, both the original backup and cartoon version are deleted from storage.
acceptanceCriteria: DELETE /users/me/profile-picture deletes profile-pictures/{uid}/original.webp using deleteIfExists() (idempotent)
DELETE /users/me/profile-picture deletes profile-pictures/{uid}/avatar.webp using deleteIfExists() (existing behavior, already present)
Both deletions complete before Firestore profilePictureUrl is removed
Endpoint still returns 204 No Content
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: The delete handler is in services/api/src/routes/users.ts at DELETE /users/me/profile-picture (lines 365-379). Currently only deletes avatar.webp. Add a second deleteIfExists() call for original.webp using the same getContainerClient('uploads').getBlockBlobClient() pattern.

userStories.title: Create migration script for existing users' profile pictures
userStories.description: As an admin, I can run a one-time migration script to convert all existing users' profile pictures to cartoon versions.
acceptanceCriteria: Create scripts/migrate-cartoon-pictures.ts as a standalone Node.js/TypeScript script
Script queries Firestore for all users with profilePictureUrl set
For each user, downloads current avatar.webp and backs it up as original.webp in blob storage
Sends the image through ReplicateCartoonService for cartoon conversion
Uploads cartoon result as avatar.webp replacing the original
Updates Firestore profilePictureUrl with a new cache-bust timestamp
Script is idempotent: skips users whose original.webp already exists in blob storage
Supports --limit N flag to process only N users (for testing)
Supports --dry-run flag to preview actions without making changes
Logs progress and errors to stdout
Processes users sequentially (to respect Replicate rate limits)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use the same Firestore and Azure Blob Storage initialization patterns from the API service (services/api/src/lib/). The script should use ts-node or tsx to run. Parse CLI args with process.argv or a simple arg parser. Follow existing script patterns in scripts/ directory.

userStories.title: Update upload timeout and loading UX for cartoon processing
userStories.description: As a user, I see clear feedback during the longer upload process that my cartoon avatar is being created, and the upload doesn't prematurely time out.
acceptanceCriteria: In apps/web/src/lib/api.ts, increase apiUploadFile timeout from 45_000 to 60_000 ms
In apps/mobile/src/lib/api.ts, increase apiUploadFile timeout from 45_000 to 60_000 ms
On web, update the profile picture upload loading text to 'Creating your cartoon avatar...' (find the upload UI in the profile/settings page)
On mobile, update the profile picture upload loading text to 'Creating your cartoon avatar...' (find the upload UI in the profile/settings screen)
User can still cancel the upload (existing AbortController behavior preserved)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Web timeout is in apps/web/src/lib/api.ts (timeoutMs = 45_000). Mobile timeout is in apps/mobile/src/lib/api.ts (timeoutMs = 45_000). Search for 'Uploading' or similar loading text in profile picture upload components on both platforms to find where to update the message.
